blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: Jonathan said it's just school too. You both say the same stuff.

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: I'm just saying. You've been up since forever.
He came and sat next to me anyway. He does that.

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: You should wear the blue one.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I haven't even opened the closet yet.

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: I know. But you should wear the blue one.
He has opinions about everything. I don't know where he got that from.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Since when do you know about clothes?

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: Since always. Mr. Cole says how you look is how people decide if they're listening before you even talk.

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: Last Tuesday. You were at Jonathan's.
I stood in front of the closet for longer than I should have. The thing about a new school is nobody knows what you normally wear. You can be whoever shows up.

blocks.prompt: Who does Larry want to walk in as today?

options.label: Someone who belongs there. Clean, intentional, nothing to prove.
Someone you can't read yet. Understated, watching.
Jonathan's kind of guy. Athletic, easy, like this is already your school.
The blue one. Larry Jr. was right.
